generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor with input current (I_M) and output torque (τ_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearwheel component transforming torque between motor and cycloidal gearbox in the first axis."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox transforming torque (τ_G → τ_out) and providing high reduction ratio for the first axis."
]

instance SINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller executing control algorithms using position/speed/acceleration inputs and outputs target/actual values."
]

instance SafetyCell : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Safety enclosure restricting operational range of first axis to [-90°, 90°] with maximum speed 75°/s."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder directly mounted on motor measuring angular position (r_M) for first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder behind gearbox measuring output angle (r_out) to reduce backlash/uncertainty via encoder difference analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance CurrentSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance MQTTStreamedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (bdd) representing structural model of first axis with Rotational and Current flow definitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (ibd) showing instance-level connections for FirstAxis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance FrictionlessGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing ideal behavior of gearbox without friction components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink Coulomb-Viscous friction model subtracted from ideal model to estimate friction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink Stribeck friction model accounting for Stribeck effect in gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink Dahl friction model for dynamic friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink LuGre friction model for advanced friction dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MotorSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink motor simulation model converting input current (I_M) to torque (τ_M)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of measured input current (I_M) streamed via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler, DashboardEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of measured output angle (r_out) streamed via MQTT from secondary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler, DashboardEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor angle (r_M) calculated from motor encoder data and dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque (τ) values computed from encoder data and manufacturer-provided robot dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DerivedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived angular velocity (ω) calculated from position encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulatedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque values (δτ_M, δτ_G, δτ_out) processed via Simulink friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStreamedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardEnabler
]

instance SimulatedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity values processed via Simulink friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStreamedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling environment for structural and behavioral modeling of robot axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelTraceabilityService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for executable friction and dynamics simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, MultiPhysicsAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, r_out) to digital shadow."
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell framework for semantic data linking and traceability between physical/virtual components."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTraceabilityService
]

instance DashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization dashboard comparing simulated vs. measured data traces (τ_M, ω_G, τ_out)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in gearbox using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ControlParameterUpdateAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated dashboard displaying simulated vs. measured data traces for deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight
]

instance ModelTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Links data points to processing components (Simulink models) and simulation models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) for explainability."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelExplainabilityInsight
]

instance MultiPhysicsAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Combines mechanical (torque, angular velocity), electrical (current), and friction-related data for holistic understanding."
    DTDFVocab:provides MultiPhysicsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static/kinetic/viscous friction components in the gearbox using analytical and data-driven models."
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of torque/velocity errors between simulated and measured data traces."
]

instance ModelExplainabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability of data points to processing components (Simulink models) and simulation models for explainability."
]

instance MultiPhysicsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Holistic understanding combining mechanical, electrical, and friction-related data analysis."
]

instance ControlParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggests adjustments to CNC algorithms for friction compensation based on identified friction characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Detects wear patterns via trend analysis of friction models and generates maintenance alerts."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, SafetyCell, SINUMERIK840DslController,
                MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram, FrictionlessGearboxModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel, MotorSimulationModel,
                MeasuredCurrentData, MeasuredOutputAngleData, DerivedMotorAngleData, DerivedTorqueData,
                DerivedAngularVelocityData, SimulatedTorqueData, SimulatedAngularVelocityData
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DS does not directly send data back to the physical system (PT) for actuation. However, the insights gained from the DS (e.g., friction model improvements) can be used to **optimize control algorithms** in the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller**, indirectly improving the PT's performance. The DS provides **visualization and analysis** (e.g., dashboards) to support decision-making for robot calibration and maintenance."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **digital shadow operates in real-time** with:
    - **Live data ingestion**: Sensor data streamed via **MQTT** with **low-latency** requirements for friction model updates.
    - **Dynamic model exchange**: Friction models (white-box/black-box) are **continuously refined** during operation to adapt to changes (e.g., wear, temperature).
    - **Synchronization**: Data traces are **time-aligned** with the physical system’s operational clock (e.g., encoder timestamps)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **digital shadow constellation** is orchestrated as:
    - **Centralized architecture**: Single DS instance for the first axis, with **modular processing components** (simulation models).
    - **Data flow pipeline**:
        1. **Sensor data ingestion** (MQTT → DS).
        2. **Processing** (Simulink models compute friction-compensated traces).
        3. **Visualization** (dashboard displays deviations).
    - **No feedback loop**: Passive data collection (no active control or DT services)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **digital shadow covers the operational phase** of the physical system, focusing on:
    - **As-operated representation**: Models the robot’s **current state** (e.g., friction characteristics under real-world conditions).
    - **Dynamic adaptation**: Supports **continuous model updates** during operation to account for **wear, tool changes, or environmental factors** (e.g., temperature).
    - **No design/manufacturing phases**: Limited to **runtime friction analysis** (no virtual commissioning or as-designed simulations)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity and validity** are ensured via:
    - **Model validation**:
        - **White-box models** (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) use **manufacturer-provided robot parameters**.
        - **Black-box models** (data-driven) are **continuously identified** from live sensor data.
    - **Uncertainty quantification**:
        - **Deviation analysis**: Compares simulated (*ˆr*) vs. measured (*r*) data traces to detect friction errors.
        - **Traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source** (sensor/encoder) and **processing method** (e.g., Simulink block).
    - **Verification**: Cross-validated using **two encoders** (motor + secondary) to reduce backlash uncertainty."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** includes:
    - **Network protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming (low-latency, lightweight).
    - **Data format**: Time-series arrays of **floating-point values** (torque, angular velocity) with **metadata** (source, timestamp).
    - **Integration**: Sensor data is **semantically linked** to SysML/Simulink models via **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for traceability."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DS is hosted on a **local or cloud-based system** (not explicitly specified in the document) that supports:
  - Real-time data processing (e.g., MQTT broker).
  - Simulation and modeling (e.g., MATLAB Simulink).
  - Visualization (e.g., dashboard for monitoring).
  The deployment is designed for **operational use**, with low latency to support real-time friction modeling and analysis."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DS is designed to integrate with:
  - **External Systems**: Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller for control improvements.
  - **Manufacturer Data**: Motor and gearbox parameters provided by MABI Robotic AG.
  - **Future Extensions**: Potential integration with other digital twins (e.g., for other robot axes or manufacturing processes) via Asset Administration Shell (AAS) or Industry 4.0 standards."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DS adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  - **Modeling**: SysML for structural and behavioral modeling, following MBSE principles.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model**: Based on the work by Kritzinger et al. [32] and Michael et al. [15].
  - **Data Streaming**: MQTT for real-time data transmission.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: For semantic interoperability and data linking.
  - **Simulation**: MATLAB Simulink for friction and dynamics modeling."
]
